title: Quantum Computing & Cryptography
description: A deep dive into how quantum computing threatens and enables new forms of cryptography, including post-quantum algorithms.

nodes.title: Quantum Entanglement
nodes.note: Particles share states across arbitrary distances — the foundation of quantum key distribution.

nodes.title: Shor's Algorithm
nodes.note: Polynomial-time integer factorization on a quantum computer. Threatens RSA and ECC.

nodes.title: Peter Shor
nodes.note: MIT mathematician who discovered the algorithm in 1994 that would eventually threaten public-key cryptography.

nodes.title: IBM Quantum
nodes.note: Operates the largest fleet of quantum computers. Their 1,121-qubit Condor processor launched in 2023.

nodes.title: NIST Post-Quantum Standards
nodes.note: NIST finalized CRYSTALS-Kyber and CRYSTALS-Dilithium as post-quantum standards in 2024.

nodes.title: When will RSA be broken?
nodes.note: Estimates range from 2030 to 2050+. Depends on error correction breakthroughs.

nodes.title: NIST PQC Standardization (2024)
nodes.note: First post-quantum cryptographic standards published, marking a critical milestone.

nodes.title: Quantum Supremacy is Overstated
nodes.note: Some researchers argue current 'quantum advantage' demos solve contrived problems with no practical value.

nodes.title: Feynman on Simulation
nodes.note: "Nature isn't classical, dammit, and if you want to make a simulation of nature, you'd better make it quantum mechanical." — Richard Feynman, 1981

nodes.title: IBM Quantum Roadmap Diagram
nodes.note: Visual showing the path from 127 qubits (Eagle, 2021) to 100,000+ qubits by 2033.

nodes.title: Über-Verschränkung (超纠缠)
nodes.note: Unicode test: A hypothetical stronger-than-entanglement quantum correlation. Ελληνικά: κβαντική. 日本語: 量子.
